originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&flags` is never used.

presenceCondition: (and (= CROSS_COMPILE false) (= WIN32 true) (= NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES true))
model:
(define-fun WIN32 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CROSS_COMPILE () Bool
  false)
reasoning: Line 38: `flags |= T_ESCAPE_SHELL_CMD` inside NEED_ENHANCED block. flags is assigned in the loop but never read — dead store. No '// ERROR' → new bug not in VBDB. The dead store occurs whenever NEED_ENHANCED=T (with CROSS_COMPILE=F to avoid infeasibility). WIN32=T here causes NEED_ENHANCED to be internally defined (L10-11), but is not itself required for the dead store — NEED_ENHANCED=T alone suffices (OS2=T would also define it). PC adds WIN32=T as an unnecessary constraint. PC is over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
